title: list-tools is a single-shot full-schema dump — no progressive disclosure (names/index -> per-tool detail); the ~54-tool payload exceeds the 50KB cap and truncates, blinding the agent's own discovery surface
description: list-tools (pi-context/src/index.ts:997) returns one JSON blob of { tools:[{name,description,parameters,sourceInfo}], active, total, activeCount } for ALL loaded tools (~54), each carrying its FULL parameters schema, then truncateHead-caps at 50KB (index.ts:1016) appending only the prose note '[Truncated: N bytes exceeds 50KB limit.]' (index.ts:1019; observed ~58KB -> truncated). Because list-tools is the harness-confined agent's PRIMARY discovery mechanism (DEC-0014 / FGAP-044), truncating it is uniquely perverse: the one tool whose job is to reveal the surface silently drops the tail of that surface, and a satisficing LLM treats the partial catalog as complete (the FGAP-089 false-completeness failure, applied to discovery itself). There is no two-level disclosure: no compact INDEX mode (tool name + one-line summary, fits under cap) and no DETAIL mode (full parameters for a named tool / subset). This is the same progressive-disclosure pattern FGAP-045 established for blocks (read-block all-or-nothing -> read-block-item + read-block-page {items,total,hasMore}), un-applied to the tool catalog. Surfaced 2026-05-25 by the user observing the truncation in a constrained-harness session.

evidence.reference: list-tools registration; builds {tools:[{name,description,parameters,sourceInfo}],...} over getAllTools()/getActiveTools(), JSON.stringify + truncateHead at :1016, prose-only '[Truncated: N bytes exceeds 50KB limit.]' at :1019. Single mode; full parameters per tool; no index/detail split.

read-block-page is the established progressive-disclosure precedent ({items,total,hasMore}, offset/limit) for blocks — the shape list-tools should mirror for the tool catalog (index page + per-tool detail).

Observed live in a constrained-harness session: list-tools truncated at 50KB (~58KB payload); the agent satisficed on the partial view and explicitly reached for a read-block-page-style index->detail for the tool catalog ('let me try read-block-page approach... no, that's for substrate blocks') before finding it absent — direct confirmation of the missing progressive disclosure this gap describes.

impact: The agent's primary (and, per FGAP-090, near-sole) discovery channel is also the payload most likely to exceed 50KB, so discovery is silently incomplete: tools past the cap are invisible to the in-pi LLM, which then hand-waves or confabulates signatures (the FGAP-092 confabulation pattern). Compounds FGAP-085 (even well-authored descriptions are dropped past the cap) and instances FGAP-089 (prose-only truncation signal). As the tool count grows, the truncation worsens.
proposed_resolution: Make list-tools two-level (progressive disclosure), mirroring read-block-page: (1) default INDEX mode returns a compact entry per tool (name + one-line summary + param-count + sourceInfo + active flag) for ALL tools — small enough to never truncate; (2) a DETAIL mode (param e.g. { tool: '<name>' } or { names: [...] }) returns the full parameters schema for the requested tool(s). Pair with FGAP-089 (emit a structured completeness signal, not a prose note) and FGAP-085 (the index surfaces descriptions, so description quality becomes load-bearing). Keep Pi-tool-only per FGAP-044's deliberate DEC-0019/0020 deviation (runtime introspection lives only in a loaded pi session; the orchestrator is served by grep/skills). Implementation routes through plan mode (source edit to index.ts).
